commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9ec3ab9a118b90f79946d645fbd6bbae113b26a1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds native Signal quote metadata for inbound-triggered replies, quote-rejection fallback, REST container field mapping, focused tests, and one Signal docs note.

PR surface: Source +156, Tests +379, Docs +1. Total +536 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the merge blocker from source inspection: live GitHub reports the PR as DIRTY/CONFLICTING, and the PR head's Signal deliverReplies path lacks current main's structured deliveredPayload/result registration. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is needed because the PR is maintainer-labeled, draft, and conflicting, and the remaining work is conflict resolution plus transport proof rather than a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: Add native Signal reply quotes
target.body:
## Summary

Signal auto-replies now preserve native Signal quote context when OpenClaw is responding to the inbound message that triggered the turn. The Signal plugin threads the inbound message timestamp, author, and body through its reply delivery path, sends quote metadata to native `signal-cli`/json-rpc, and maps the same quote fields to the Signal REST container send API. If quote metadata is missing, explicitly opted out, points at a different reply target, or is rejected by the backend, OpenClaw falls back to a normal Signal message instead of dropping the reply.

## What Changed

- Inbound Signal handling records the inbound timestamp as the current reply target and carries native quote context into reply delivery.
- Signal text and media auto-replies include `replyToId`, `replyToAuthor`, and `replyToBody` only when they match the triggering inbound message.
- `replyToCurrent: false` and mismatched explicit reply targets suppress native quote metadata, preserving existing command/status no-thread behavior.
- Native send retries without quote metadata when Signal rejects quote fields, while receipts preserve the first-class `replyToId` and record whether native quote send used `sent` or `fallback` status.
- Signal REST container mode translates quote metadata to the `/v2/send` snake_case fields.
- Signal docs now describe native quote behavior and fallback.

## Proof

Behavioral proof:

- Focused tests cover native quote send, fallback retry, receipt metadata, REST container quote fields, inbound context wiring, text replies, media replies, mismatched reply-target suppression, and `replyToCurrent: false` suppression.
- Live Signal proof used a local Signal REST/json-rpc linked device. A non-self inbound event in the Testing realm group was captured, `extensions/signal/src/send.ts` sent one quoted proof reply with `replyToId`, `replyToAuthor`, and `replyToBody`, Signal accepted the send with `deliveredMessageIdPresent=true`, and Jesse visually confirmed the reply appeared in Signal.

Automated checks:

- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts extensions/signal/src/client-container.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor.tool-result.sends-tool-summaries-responseprefix.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts` passed: 5 files, 126 tests.
- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- Targeted Blacksmith Testbox changed gate `tbx_01kwaxm04x06337mak6sxmhe8v` passed `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed` on the 11-file Signal diff; lanes were extensions, extensionTests, and docs.
- Native review pass on `651462424c5` completed clean with no accepted/actionable findings.

Screenshots are not included because this is a backend channel transport change with no OpenClaw-rendered UI surface.
target.author: jesse-merhi
